title: strychnos toxifera
intro: Strychnos toxifera, also known as the poison nut tree or calabar bean tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Loganiaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Strychnos toxifera include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a purgative and diuretic. Herbal preparations of Strychnos toxifera can be found in the form of extracts, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various ailments.
